news.title: Intel Stock Rockets on Apple Partnership
news.summary: Shares in Intel are up nearly 10% in premarket trading after President Trump said Apple agreed to work with the tech giant to design and build chips in the U.S. Apple and Intel previously reached a preliminary agreement for Intel to manufacture some chips for Apple devices after more than a year of intense talks, The Wall Street Journal reported in May.

news.title: Oracle (ORCL) And Microsoft Cloud Deal Reportedly Fell Apart Over Security Rules
news.summary: Talks for a potential $3b+ cloud infrastructure leasing partnership between Oracle and Microsoft reportedly fell apart over security certification requirements for U.S. government workloads. Reports indicated that Oracle did not agree to add FedRAMP security certification, which is commonly required for regulated public sector contracts. news.title: 2 Reasons to Like CVX and 1 to Stay Skeptical
news.summary: Chevron has had an impressive run over the past six months as its shares have beaten the S&P 500 by 9.5%. The stock now trades at $177.80, marking a 20.4% gain. This was partly thanks to its solid quarterly results, and the run-up might have investors contemplating their next move.

news.title: Seeing Machines, Quantum Blockchain, London BTC, Foresight Environmental Infrastructure
news.summary: Seeing Machines Ltd (AIM, OTC, FRA) has secured a $31 million expansion of an existing programme with a major European carmaker. The deal extends its driver and occupant monitoring technology into additional vehicle models, with production due to begin in 2026. Quantum Blockchain Technologies PLC (AIM, FRA) says its BlocKeeper subsidiary has now been incorporated in Malta. The business plans to build a capital-light Bitcoin virtual mining operation by acquiring and managing hash rate from industry participants. London BTC Company Ltd (LSE, OTCQB) has added a third project to its US gold hedge strategy with the Teep Gold-Silver Project in Nevada. Early sampling returned high-grade gold and silver results, with follow-up work now planned to define drill targets. Foresight Environmental Infrastructure (LSE) has appointed Stephanie Coxon as its next chair. She succeeds Ed Warner in September after four years in the role, during which the company completed a rebrand and delivered dividend growth.
